title: Bash history expansion (!) conflicts with agence ! command prefix
description: Bash history expansion treats ! as a special character that expands to previous commands. When a user types `!bash`, bash replaces it with the last command starting with 'bash' before agence ever receives the argument. This breaks the entire !command system.
root_cause: Bash interactive shells enable history expansion by default (`set -H`). The `!` character is expanded at parse time — before any script or function sees it. This is a shell-level problem, not an agence-level problem.
solution: Add `set +H` to .agencerc and ~/.bashrc to disable history expansion in agence sessions. The `!` prefix is reclaimed for agence system commands. Users who need bash history expansion can use Ctrl+R instead.
lesson_summary: When using ! as a command prefix in bash, you MUST disable history expansion with `set +H`. This cannot be done inside the called script — it must be done in the calling shell's profile.

code_pattern.fix_agencerc: set +H  # Disable history expansion in agence sessions
code_pattern.fix_bashrc: set +H  # Added to ~/.bashrc for VSCode terminal compatibility
code_pattern.verify: echo '!bash'  # Should print literal !bash, not expand to last bash command

implementation.file1: bin/.agencerc - Added set +H after shell detection
implementation.file2: ~/.bashrc - Added set +H for VSCode integrated terminal

references: codex/agents/peers/agent.md - @peers consensus that identified the fix

consensus.winner: peer_1 — set +H in profile (simplest, most reliable)
